title: Streaking Bacteria on an Agar Plate from a Glycerol Stock
author: Cristian Riccio
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.5xig7ke

steps.title: Transfer the glycerol stock onto dry...
steps.instruction: Transfer the glycerol stock onto dry ice next to a flame.

steps.title: Rub the loop in the glycerol stock
steps.instruction: Rub the loop in the glycerol stock.

steps.title: Streak the bacteri in zigzag over half the agar plate
steps.instruction: Streak the bacteri in zigzag over half the agar plate.

steps.title: Sterilise the loop in the flame
steps.instruction: Sterilise the loop in the flame.

steps.title: Zigzag over half the plate which...
steps.instruction: Zigzag over half the plate which overlaps with the first half by a quarter of a plate.

steps.title: Zigzag over half the plate which...
steps.instruction: Zigzag over half the plate which overlaps with the second half by a quarter of a plate.

steps.title: Incube overnight at the temperature which...
steps.instruction: Incube overnight at the temperature which is optimal for bacterial growth, 37 C for E. coli.

steps.title: The next day, you should have...
steps.instruction: The next day, you should have single colonies visible on a quarter of the plate or half the plate.
